claim_text: A Linear issue requires only a title and a status; all other properties (priority, estimate, label, assignee, relations, sub-issues) are optional by design.
label: CONFIRMED

anchor.quote: Issues are required to have a title and status – all other properties and relations are optional. This makes it quick to create issues and cuts down on unnecessary work (see Write Issues Not User Stories).

claim_text: Linear workflow statuses belong to one of five categories (backlog, unstarted, started, completed, canceled); the AI Agency workspace's six statuses map onto them, with Duplicate being a distinct status type.
label: CONFIRMED

anchor.quote: list_issue_statuses -> Backlog(type:backlog), Todo(unstarted), In Progress(started), Done(completed), Canceled(canceled), Duplicate(duplicate)

notes: Doc category model (S001/S007, tier2) + direct observation of the 6 live statuses (S031, tier1).

claim_text: Priority is a fixed five-value enum (No priority, Low, Medium, High, Urgent); Linear deliberately refuses custom or more-granular priorities.
label: CONFIRMED

anchor.quote: Issues can be set to No priority, Low, Medium, High, or Urgent. We don't have the option to set custom priorities or more granular priorities since it's easy to get carried away with specificity.

claim_text: Linear's estimate point scale maps T-shirt sizes to Fibonacci: XS=1, S=2, M=3, L=5, XL=8, XXL=13, XXXL=21; estimates are opt-in per team and large estimates signal a need to split.
label: CONFIRMED

anchor.quote: T-shirt sizes have the following point values: No priority (0), XS (1), S(2), M (3), L (5), XL (8), XXL (13), XXXL (21)

claim_text: Linear relations are a closed set: blocks/blocked-by (Blocking/Blocked), related, duplicate, and parent/sub-issue; they are first-class filter categories.
label: CONFIRMED

anchor.quote: Issue relations Blocked, Blocking, Related, Parent, Sub-issue, Duplicate

claim_text: Sub-issues are for work too large for one issue but too small for a project; they inherit the parent's team, priority, and project (not labels), and support optional parent/sub auto-close automations.
label: CONFIRMED

anchor.quote: Consider creating sub-issues when a set of work is too large to be a single issue but too small to be a project. ... Sub-issues inherit the parent issue's team, priority, and project. ... Labels are not inherited.

notes: Detail page (S008) + conceptual model (S001) both vendor-official.

claim_text: Projects group issues toward a specific, time-bound deliverable; initiatives are a manually curated list of projects expressing company objectives over long timelines (leadership tooling).
label: CONFIRMED

anchor.quote: Projects group issues towards a specific, time-bound deliverable, like launching a new feature. ... An Initiative is a manually curated list of projects with an accompanying document.

claim_text: Project/initiative status updates are structured reports = a health indicator (On track / At risk / Off track) plus rich-text body, posted by the lead/owner on a cadence, distinct from issue comments.
label: CONFIRMED

anchor.quote: They consist of a health indicator ... Select a health indicator—On track, At risk, or Off track—to represent the current state of the Initiative or Project.

notes: Updates doc (S016) + initiatives doc health rollup (S011), both vendor-official.

claim_text: The Linear Method's principles are Build for the creators, Purpose-built, Create momentum (don't sprint), and Meaningful direction; practices include working in n-week cycles, keeping a manageable backlog, mixing feature/quality work, naming one owner per issue/project, and writing project specs.
section: 2. The Linear Method — and its lean-solo subset
label: CONFIRMED

anchor.quote: Create momentum – don't sprint ... Work in n-week cycles ... Keep a manageable backlog ... Specify project and issue owners ... the responsibility should lie with a single person.

notes: Method intro (S009) + the keystone Method essay (S010), both linear.app/method canonical.

claim_text: Linear's keystone issue-writing doctrine: write issues that describe a concrete task or problem with a clear, defined outcome; if it is not a task it does not belong in the issue tracker; user stories are an anti-pattern.
section: 2. The Linear Method — and its lean-solo subset
label: CONFIRMED

anchor.quote: An issue should describe a task with a clear, defined outcome. ... If it's not a task, then it doesn't belong in the issue tracker.

claim_text: For a lean solo workspace with no cycles and no initiatives, cycle/sprint planning, WIP-limit machinery, and initiative hierarchy are overkill; the applicable subset of the Method is: write concrete-problem issues, one owner, manageable backlog, mix feature/quality work, short specs.
section: 2. The Linear Method — and its lean-solo subset
label: CONFIRMED

anchor.quote: WIP limits make blockers and bottlenecks visible. Teams can swarm around blocking issues [a multi-person team-flow tool, Atlassian]; workspace runs 0 cycles / 0 initiatives (S031, direct observation).

notes: Tier-1/2 support: S009 Method (t2), S030 Atlassian WIP (t2), S031 direct obs (t1). Cascade: s12=3, s1=1, c=0 -> credibility 1. The S028 contradiction (Morgen, tier3) is real but does not lower Tier-1/2 credibility; it is surfaced in the report's Contradictions section. The label reflects the model/workspace fact (cycles+WIP+initiative machinery is multi-person tooling, and this workspace runs none), not a claim that cycles are universally wrong.

claim_text: Linear's published good-issue pattern: title states the problem/outcome concretely; the description states problem, desired behavior, scope, and explicitly what NOT to change, and points to existing patterns to reuse — this is Linear's definition-of-done/acceptance-criteria pattern for agent-consumable issues.
section: 3. How to write an excellent Linear issue (the good issue checklist)
label: CONFIRMED

anchor.quote: Well-scoped: Issue search excludes issues from archived projects, even when Include archived is enabled. ... Do not modify search ranking, pagination, or project search behavior.

notes: Vendor-official (S020) + Sentry eng playbook (S033, tier2). Tier-4 community example moved out of support (cited in report prose only).

claim_text: Decision boundary: a single concrete task is an issue (with sub-issues if needed); a specific time-bound deliverable grouping many issues is a project; a curated portfolio of projects toward a company objective is an initiative. For lean-solo, most work is an issue, occasional multi-week builds are projects, and initiatives are not used.
section: 3. How to write an excellent Linear issue (the good issue checklist)
label: CONFIRMED

anchor.quote: Sometimes an issue grows so large it's more appropriate to turn it into a project instead. ... Projects group issues towards a specific, time-bound deliverable ... An Initiative is a manually curated list of projects.

notes: Workspace state (S031: 0 initiatives) anchors the lean-solo carve-out.

claim_text: Assigning an issue to an agent is delegation, not ownership transfer: the human assignee remains responsible for completion and stays primary assignee while the agent is an additional contributor.
label: CONFIRMED

anchor.quote: Agents are not traditional assignees. Assigning an issue to an agent triggers delegation—the agent acts on the issue, but the human teammate remains responsible for its completion.

claim_text: Linear instructs agents that comments are editable and unreliable to read back from; agents should rely on Agent Activities (frozen-in-time snapshots) and signal completion via a structured final activity (response/elicitation/error), not by mutating the spec.
section: 5. The Linear MCP server surface + agent-ops best practices
label: CONFIRMED

anchor.quote: Comments may not be reliable to read from, as they are editable and may have changed since your agent's last run. Instead, rely on Agent Activities as these are frozen-in-time snapshots of user input.

claim_text: Agent Session state in Linear is managed automatically from the agent's emitted activities (no manual state management); activities are typed thought / action / elicitation / response / error, with a prompt type reserved for user input the agent cannot create.
label: CONFIRMED

anchor.quote: Session state is visible to users, and updated automatically based on the agent's emitted activities. No manual state management is required.

claim_text: The Linear MCP server exposes a curated, task-completion-oriented tool surface (list_/get_/create_/update_/save_ families + search_documentation), not a 1:1 GraphQL mapping; the live linear-server toolset uses save_* idempotent-upsert naming.
section: 5. The Linear MCP server surface + agent-ops best practices
label: PROBABLY TRUE

anchor.quote: The list tools provide curated parameter sets rather than exposing Linear's full GraphQL filter capabilities. ... [live tools observed: get_issue, save_issue, list_issues, save_comment, save_status_update, save_project, save_milestone]

notes: Audit (S022, Tier3 C) + direct observation (S031, Tier1 A). Cascade: s12=1 (only S031 is tier<=2), s1=1, c=0 -> credibility 2.

claim_text: Agent-ops routing rule: edit the issue description only to author/refine a spec and NEVER overwrite a human-authored description; post comments/activities for progress, findings, questions, and decisions; post a project status update (health + body) only for project-level stakeholder progress.
section: 5. The Linear MCP server surface + agent-ops best practices
label: CONFIRMED

anchor.quote: [S017] rely on Agent Activities as frozen-in-time snapshots; [S015] Linear Agent can post, edit, and delete its own comments; [S016] status update = health indicator + rich text body for project progress; [S032] comments are editable, the comment field is the @Linear entry point.

notes: Derived routing rule synthesized from four vendor-official sources.

claim_text: Linear recommends automating status transitions over manual ticket management; the agent state pattern is acknowledge on start, move to In Progress, post progress as comments/activities, and seek human confirmation before marking Done.
section: 5. The Linear MCP server surface + agent-ops best practices
label: CONFIRMED

anchor.quote: we recommend using integrations or automations to update issue status to avoid the need to 'manage tickets'. ... [S014] delegation: the human remains responsible for completion.

notes: Vendor principle (S001) + delegation-responsibility doctrine (S014). The community work-on-item.md implementation (S018, tier4) is cited in report prose only, not as factual support.

claim_text: Blockers should be signaled via a blocked-by relation to the blocking issue (filterable, surfaces in views), not only as prose in a comment.
section: 5. The Linear MCP server surface + agent-ops best practices
label: CONFIRMED

anchor.quote: Issue relations Blocked, Blocking, Related, Parent, Sub-issue, Duplicate [are first-class filter categories]; [S001] You can create relations between issues to mark issues as blocked or blocking.

notes: Relations are first-class/filterable (S002) and a documented issue capability (S001); both vendor-official. Hookdeck (S023, tier3) and the elicitation practice corroborate in prose.

claim_text: The AI Agency workspace has one team, six issue statuses, and a single Type label group with seven children, plus three orphan default labels (Feature, Bug, Improvement) outside any group that duplicate the Type Bug.
label: PROBABLY TRUE

anchor.quote: labels: [Type/Admin, Type/Bug, Type/Réunion, Type/Veille, Type/Conseil, Type/Contenu, Type/IA-Dev] + [Feature, Bug, Improvement] (no parent group)

notes: Single direct observation (S031, Tier1 A). Cascade: s12=1, s1=1, c=0 -> credibility 2 PROBABLY TRUE. Primary direct observation; the orphan-label hygiene gap is an actionable finding.

claim_text: Coding sessions delegating an issue spin up a Claude Code or Codex session whose workspace default model is Claude Opus 4.8; the feature launched 2026-06-11.
label: POSSIBLY TRUE

anchor.quote: Our default model (tracked under Auto) is Claude Opus 4.8. ... On our launch date of June 11th, 2026

notes: Single vendor-official source (S020, Tier2 B). Cascade: s12=1, s1=0, c=0 -> credibility 3 POSSIBLY TRUE.
